label: Game — Fruit Slicer (swipe)
html: click + drag to swipe through fruits · 3 misses = game over · click again to restart
hatchfile:
name    = "fruit-slicer"
version = "0.1.0"
entry   = "main"

[dependencies]
# Local override: bundles a Sprite.rotation + Renderer2D.drawSpriteRotated_
# extension on top of the published @hatch:gpu source. The user-level
# entry MUST come before @hatch:game so the dep walker sees-and-skips
# the transitive 0.2.4 pin in @hatch:game's manifest.
"@hatch:gpu"  = { url = "./hatch-gpu.hatch" }
"@hatch:game" = "0.2.6"
"@hatch:json" = "0.1.0"

label: DOM + async scheduler
html:
hello, wren

edit me from Wren via Dom.setText.

label: JSON parse + assertions
html:
JSON demo

output is in the console pane.
wren:
import "@hatch:json"   for JSON
import "@hatch:assert" for Expect

var text =
  "{\"package\": \"wlift\"," +
  " \"version\": \"0.4.0\"," +
  " \"targets\": [\"x86_64\", \"aarch64\", \"wasm32\"]," +
  " \"jit\": true}"

var obj = JSON.parse(text)

System.print("name     = %(obj["package"])")
System.print("version  = %(obj["version"])")
System.print("targets  = %(obj["targets"].join(", "))")
System.print("jit      = %(obj["jit"])")

Expect.that(obj["package"]).toBe("wlift")
Expect.that(obj["targets"].count).toBe(3)
Expect.that(obj["jit"]).toBe(true)

// Round-trip through encode/decode.
var encoded = JSON.encode({"hello": "world", "n": 42})
var back = JSON.parse(encoded)
Expect.that(back["n"]).toBe(42)
System.print("✓ round-trip: %(encoded)")
